tasks.title: PTY session manager — handoff, countdown, idx injection
tasks.acceptance_criteria: SessionManager detects session type, injects idx banner every 3 turns, triggers handoff+countdown+restart when threshold exceeded; countdown callable works standalone

tasks.title: CLI cmd_shell — PTY I/O relay loop
tasks.acceptance_criteria: agentflow shell --command claude launches claude under PTY; IDX banners every 3 turns; handoff fires at threshold; terminal restored on exit

tasks.title: PTY — per-turn output token tracking + verbosity compliance signal
tasks.acceptance_criteria: verbosity_log.jsonl accumulates per-turn output token counts; banner fires on verbose turns; shadow analyzer reads mean/p90 vs 150-token target

tasks.title: Provider-keyed rate calibration files: rate_calibration_claude.json / rate_calibration_gemini.json
read_check.py: skip block on empty idx + compress output message
tasks.description: Two fixes to read_check.py PreToolUse hook. (1) False-positive block: when .idx exists but is 0 bytes (file <50 lines or no indexable sections), hook still fires sys.exit(1) — add short-circuit after strip() so empty idx allows full read through. (2) Verbose output: current 3-line error message (~50 tokens) fires into model context on every blocked read; compress to single telegraphic line.

tasks.title: UserPromptSubmit hook — .idx reminder injection (replaces PTY stdin)
tasks.description: Wire up agentflow/hooks/idx_reminder.py as a UserPromptSubmit hook in .claude/settings.json. Hook fires after user submits each message (input field already cleared — injection invisible). Every 3rd turn outputs [IDX] reminder text; Claude receives it as context without user seeing it in the input field. Add tests/hooks/test_idx_reminder.py covering: counter increments on each call, reminder output on turn 3/6/9, no output on turns 1/2/4, counter file created if absent, counter resets gracefully on corrupt file. Remove PTY stdin .idx injection from session_manager.py (_inject_idx_banner call site in the every-3-turns path) — hook supersedes it. PTY Read-event detection (T-052) stays: it is reactive (fires on detected Read calls), hook is proactive (periodic recalibration). Both complement; stdin injection is the only part removed.

tasks.title: PostToolUse hook: block Write/Edit when file exceeds line limit
tasks.description: New hook agentflow/hooks/size_check.py — PostToolUse on Write and Edit. After each write, read the modified file and check line count against per-category limits: tests/ → 350, commands/ → 150, all other .py → 250. Stubs excluded: files whose content is >50% pass/ellipsis lines skip the check. On violation: print single telegraphic message and exit(1) to block next tool call — "FILE TOO LARGE: <rel_path> is <N> lines (limit <L>) — split by responsibility boundary before proceeding." On pass: exit(0), no output. Register under PostToolUse for both Write and Edit matchers in .claude/settings.json alongside write_indexer. Add tests/hooks/test_size_check.py covering: under-limit passes, over-limit blocks, correct limit per category, stub exemption, non-.py files skipped.

tasks.title: Bake verbosity target into skill prompts as standing instruction
tasks.description: Add a consistent verbosity target to oracle.md and orchestrate.md as a permanent standing instruction, not an optional argument. oracle.md: promote the Phase 2 verbosity note ("≤3 sentences per exchange") to a top-level instruction visible before any phase executes — add "≤3 sentences (~150 tokens) per response; expand only if user asks" near the opening. Remove the duplicate in Phase 2. orchestrate.md: extend the existing "## Verbosity rules" section to include the token target — "≤3 sentences (~150 tokens) per orchestrator status message". Both changes must survive context growth (instruction must appear early in the file, not only in a late section). No changes to handoff.md, drift.md, or worker prompts — those are single-purpose and do not have multi-turn verbosity risk.

tasks.title: Orchestrate skill — structured PTY signals + current_round.json
tasks.description: Enable PTY to track task lifecycle deterministically. (1) commands/claude/orchestrate.md: at round start, write .agentflow/current_round.json with {round_id, task_ids, estimated_lines_per_task, file_counts_per_task, timestamp}. (2) Before spawning each worker, print AGENTFLOW_TASK_START:<task_id> to stdout. (3) After each worker completes, print AGENTFLOW_TASK_COMPLETE:<task_id>. (4) After all round tasks complete, print AGENTFLOW_ROUND_COMPLETE. (5) commands/gemini/skills/orchestrate/SKILL.md: same four signals. (6) Tests: test_orchestrate_skill.py — assert all four signal strings present in Claude and Gemini skill content; assert current_round.json write step present.

tasks.title: read_check.py — block large-range reads that bypass idx enforcement
tasks.description: read_check.py currently blocks reads with no offset/limit when an idx exists. Gap: reads with offset≈0 + large limit (e.g. offset=7, limit=168 on a 174-line file) pass unblocked despite covering nearly the full file. Fix: (1) When offset and limit are both present, count total lines in file (stdlib only). (2) Compute coverage = limit / (total_lines - offset). (3) If coverage > threshold (default 0.60) AND idx exists → exit 1 with message: "Large-range read (N/M lines, X%) — use idx to target specific sections". (4) If file < 50 lines (no idx expected) → skip check. (5) Threshold configurable via AGENTFLOW_READ_COVERAGE_THRESHOLD env var. (6) Tests: tests/hooks/test_read_check.py — assert exit 1 on large-range read with idx present; assert exit 0 on targeted read (small limit); assert exit 0 on file < 50 lines; assert exit 0 when no idx exists regardless of range.

tasks.title: PTY session_manager — ROUND_COMPLETE detection + token-floor handoff
tasks.description: Replace pure token-threshold handoff with deterministic round-boundary handoff. (1) session_manager.py: scan stdout for AGENTFLOW_ROUND_COMPLETE. On detection: (a) read .agentflow/current_round.json to confirm round closed, (b) check accumulated_tokens vs configurable floor (shell.handoff_token_floor_pct, default 0.30 of threshold). (2) If above floor: call trigger_handoff() — clean boundary, no mid-task interrupt. (3) If below floor: log round-complete-low-tokens event to verbosity_log.jsonl, reset round state, continue. Token threshold remains as a safety net if ROUND_COMPLETE is never emitted. (4) Tests: test_session_manager.py — mock ROUND_COMPLETE in PTY stdout; assert handoff triggered when tokens above floor; assert handoff skipped and continued when below floor.

tasks.title: PTY session_manager — per-task token bracketing → task_token_log.jsonl
tasks.description: Collect empirical per-task token data to power the future estimator. (1) session_manager.py: on AGENTFLOW_TASK_START:<task_id>, snapshot tokenizer accumulator value as task_start_tokens. (2) On AGENTFLOW_TASK_COMPLETE:<task_id>, compute delta = current_accumulated - task_start_tokens; read estimated_lines and file_count for this task_id from current_round.json. (3) Append to ~/.agentflow/task_token_log.jsonl: {task_id, session_type, token_delta, estimated_lines, file_count, timestamp}. File created if absent. (4) Tests: test_session_manager.py — emit TASK_START + TASK_COMPLETE signals; assert task_token_log.jsonl entry written with correct delta and metadata.

tasks.title: Evaluate headroom-ai library — integration feasibility for PTY + M5
tasks.description: Research spike: determine what headroom-ai actually does before designing integration. (1) pip install headroom-ai[all]; run `headroom wrap claude --help` and explore CLI flags. (2) Determine mechanism: subprocess wrapper, HTTP proxy, ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL intercept, or other — and whether it conflicts with PTY stdin/stdout interception. (3) Understand compress(): what algorithm, lossless or lossy, token savings estimate. (4) Understand SharedContext: in-memory or persistent? Cross-process? How does cross-provider sharing work? (5) Check whether headroom exposes per-session compression metrics readable by a report generator. (6) Write findings to .agentflow/headroom_eval.md: mechanism, composability verdict, savings estimate, SharedContext design implications for M5, recommended integration approach or DEFER if incompatible.

tasks.title: Fix verbosity signal reliability — incremental writes + reminder hook
tasks.description: verbosity_log.jsonl only written in _on_session_exit (batched, last-10-turns) — sessions restarted via /clear (not a real process exit) never reach this path, silently losing all verbosity data; confirmed zero oracle-tagged entries despite completed oracle sessions. Also T-052/T-055 marked MERGED but the 'proactive verbosity banner' was never implemented (_verbosity_last_inject set once, never read; should_inject_banner absent from code and tests) — dead state. Fix: (1) session_manager.py — write each entry to verbosity_log.jsonl incrementally at turn-boundary detection (same site as _turn_output_history.append), not batched at exit; remove dead _verbosity_last_inject/_initial_banner_sent if superseded by (2). (2) New hook agentflow/hooks/verbosity_reminder.py — separate file from idx_reminder.py (one-concern-per-file convention), own counter (~/.agentflow/verbosity_turn_counter), fires every 1-2 turns (tighter than idx's 3) reminding to keep responses ≤ 3 sentences. Register as new entry under UserPromptSubmit in .claude/settings.json. (3) Tests: tests/shell/test_session_manager.py — assert incremental write fires at turn boundary without requiring exit; tests/hooks/test_verbosity_reminder.py — mirror test_idx_reminder.py structure, assert own counter + cadence.

tasks.title: Size-limit enforcement gap — Gemini writes + pre-existing violations uncovered
tasks.description: T-058's size_check.py is Claude-Code-specific (PostToolUse hook); it never fires for (a) files that existed before Claude's Write/Edit tool touched them (e.g. agentflow.py), or (b) Gemini CLI writes — no equivalent hook mechanism wired for Gemini. (1) Add standalone agentflow/hooks/size_audit.py — walks repo, applies same per-category limits as size_check.py (tests/->350, commands/->150, other .py->250, stub-exempt), reports every current violation. (2) Wire as a manual/orchestrator-invoked sweep (python3 agentflow/hooks/size_audit.py) callable at milestone boundaries by either provider's orchestrate skill — not a live hook, since no PostToolUse-equivalent event exists for Gemini CLI today. (3) Investigate whether Gemini CLI supports an analogous post-write hook; if yes, wire a size_check.py equivalent; if no, document the gap in design_status.md and rely on the periodic sweep. Tests: tests/hooks/test_size_audit.py — fixture repo with known violations, assert correct per-category detection.

tasks.title: Spike — can headroom's proxy auto-capture 5hr/weekly usage windows?
tasks.description: Research spike: orchestrate.md's rate-pacing protocol currently asks the user to manually run `/usage` and report 5hr/weekly % + reset times + caps for both claude and agy (design_status.md #57). Determine whether headroom's local HTTP proxy (T-074) can capture this automatically instead. NOTE: per-request token usage (input/output/cache) is already solved via existing JSONL/DB parsing (usage_parser.py) — this spike is scoped ONLY to the 5hr/weekly window %, reset_min, and cap fields that `/usage` displays. (1) Run `headroom wrap claude`, trigger `/usage` inside the session, and passively observe proxy traffic (headroom debug/verbose logging, or a temporary logging shim) to determine: does the underlying network call for `/usage` route through ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL (i.e. through the local proxy), or does it hit a different host directly? Only observe traffic the client already sends normally — do not attempt to authenticate to or probe undocumented endpoints directly. (2) If it routes through the proxy: inspect whether the response body/headers contain parseable window %/reset/cap fields; note exact shape if so. (3) Repeat for `headroom wrap agy` + agy's `/usage` — treat as fully separate from T-074's compression-savings verification (design_status.md #92 marks that UNVERIFIED; this spike tests usage-window visibility specifically, not compression). (4) Write findings to .agentflow/headroom_usage_eval.md: mechanism confirmed or not (per provider), feasibility verdict, and if not feasible, explicit recommendation to keep the current manual-ask + ledger-anchored derivation (design_status.md #57) as-is. No code changes — spike only, same shape as T-071.

tasks.title: Harden gate-file reads against Headroom staleness — skill rule + invocation audit
tasks.description: Oracle re-spar 2026-07-01 (Headroom incident): a live /orchestrate session reported garbled-looking Read/Grep results for design_status.md and rate_calibration_claude.json, correctly refused to act on them, and paused. Root cause traced in the installed headroom package, not guesswork: headroom/config.py's DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_TOOLS already excludes Read/Glob/Grep/Write/Edit from ContentRouter compression, and cli.py's `headroom wrap claude` invocation (cmd_shell, ~line 118-120) passes no flags, so that protection is intact by default. The live risk is headroom/transforms/read_lifecycle.py's ReadLifecycleConfig (compress_stale=True by default), which runs independent of tool exclusion and rewrites a prior turn's Read of a file into a `[Read content stale ... re-read the file]` marker once that file is later Written/Edited — a pattern AgentFlow's own handoff/calibration tooling triggers constantly on design_status.md/tasks.json/rate_calibration_claude.json within a single long-running skill session. Two-part fix. (A) commands/claude/oracle.md + commands/claude/orchestrate.md: add a standing rule near each skill's gate-check steps (design_status.md UNRESOLVED scan, tasks.json status reads, rate-calibration reads) — always issue a fresh same-turn Read before using a gate file's contents for a decision; if a Headroom stale/superseded marker or any garbled/truncated-looking result appears on a gate file, re-read immediately and never parse the marker text as data. (B) tests/test_headroom_invocation.py (new): a static assertion over agentflow/cli.py's cmd_shell that the headroom cmd_args construction contains no flags beyond `["headroom", "wrap", cmd]` — fails loudly if `--intercept-tool-results` or a non-default `--mode` is ever added without a corresponding design_status.md decision, since either would defeat DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_TOOLS's protection for Read/Grep.

tasks.title: Fix ContentRouter mode default — cli.py sets HEADROOM_MODE=cache; tag-protect hook-injected reminders
tasks.description: Oracle sparring 2026-07-01 ("ContentRouter mode default"): root cause of the T-079 incident traced to headroom's installed source (proxy/modes.py, server.py, transforms/content_router.py). `headroom wrap` with no HEADROOM_MODE set resolves via normalize_proxy_mode(mode, default="token") to token mode, which sets protect_recent_reads_fraction=0.3 — overriding the library's own safe default (0.0 = protect all DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_TOOLS output unconditionally). Under token mode, excluded-tool (Read/Grep/Write/Edit) results older than ~30% of the conversation fall through to SmartCrusher compression despite being "excluded". Fix: cli.py's cmd_shell sets HEADROOM_MODE=cache explicitly alongside HEADROOM_WORKSPACE_DIR so the protective 0.0 default holds regardless of session length. Belt-and-suspenders: hook-injected idx_reminder/verbosity_reminder text isn't a tool_result block (can't rely on exclude_tools) — wrap in a custom XML tag (protected verbatim via headroom's tag_protector, compress_tagged_content=False default) so hook reminders survive compression the same way tool_result exclusions do.

tasks.title: A/B test verbosity control savings — replace assumed 600-token baseline with measured data
tasks.description: Oracle sparring 2026-07-01: report_builder.py:54 computes verbosity_savings = sum(max(0, 600 - output_tokens)) across the full lifetime verbosity_log.jsonl (5,351+ turns since project start), where 600 is architecture.md's modeled, never-measured estimate of no-hook output length. That unvalidated per-turn baseline is summed with real measured savings (file_reads_saved) into total_saved, dominating the reported percentage (~79% of the headline figure) despite being an assumption, not a measurement. Build a controlled comparison: gate agentflow/hooks/verbosity_reminder.py behind an env var so it can be disabled; run N comparable sessions/tasks with the hook off and N with it on, capture output_tokens for both arms via session_manager.py's existing per-turn logging (tag entries with an arm/session_type so they're distinguishable in verbosity_log.jsonl). Compute measured mean/p90 for each arm; replace the hardcoded 600 constant in report_builder.py with the measured hook-off baseline (config-driven, not a magic number), and surface sample size + confidence interval next to the savings figure so small-sample runs aren't presented as precise. Also fix the scope mismatch: verbosity_savings currently sums the entire lifetime log while file_reads_saved is scoped to current shadow_reads.jsonl entries — align both to the same reporting window before summing into total_saved.

tasks.title: Fix headroom compression data source in report_builder.py
tasks.description: build_report() falls back to ~/.headroom/headroom.db (SQLite via headroom.storage.create_storage) when project-local .headroom/headroom.db is absent — but the installed headroom-ai proxy actually persists this project's telemetry to .headroom/proxy_savings.json (JSON ledger; 2026-07-02 snapshot: 1,027 requests, 9,035,421 lifetime tokens_saved). get_summary_stats() against the SQLite path returns 0 requests, so compression_savings is permanently 0 despite real measured savings existing.

tasks.title: Split shadow-waste vs real-savings labeling in report output
tasks.description: "Savings Breakdown by Strategy" mixes two different metrics under one label. idx/no-reread/indexing-gap come from get_bucketed_stats and measure shadow *waste* (tokens lost to unimplemented or violated optimizations) — 0 for idx is the correct/good outcome once read_check.py enforces compliance (realized idx savings are already counted separately in total_saved via file_reads_saved, not in this bucket). state-docs is a raw read-volume figure, not a waste or savings number. verbosity and compression are actual realized savings. Presenting all six under one "Savings Breakdown" header reads as "idx saved 0 tokens" when it means "0 tokens wasted — compliance enforced upstream." Fix: split into two sections in both console output and dashboard_template.html — "Waste Avoided (shadow, lower is better)" for idx/no-reread/indexing-gap, and "Real Savings Realized" for state-docs volume (relabeled as volume, not savings), verbosity, compression, and the total_saved headline. Depends on T-082 (compression numbers should be real before relabeling the section that surfaces them).

tasks.title: Recover headroom compression savings lost to cache mode's strict prefix freeze
tasks.description: T-080 set HEADROOM_MODE=cache to fix the T-079 corruption incident (stale excluded-tool output falling through to compression). Root-caused via oracle re-spar 2026-07-02: is_cache_mode() in headroom/proxy/handlers/anthropic.py:911-915 unconditionally overrides frozen_message_count via _strict_previous_turn_frozen_count() — freezing every message except the current turn, for every tool type, not just the Read/Grep/Write/Edit set T-080's commit message described. Evidence from .headroom/logs/proxy.log: pre-cutover (old default/token-mode session) 649/882 requests (74%) compressed, saving 8,960,769 tokens; post-cutover (cache mode) 8/920 requests (<1%, all in the ~2min transition window) — steady state since is transforms=none on literally every request. Net effect: cache mode didn't narrow compression to protect docs, it disabled ContentRouter for the entire conversation history except the newest message, killing ~all real headroom savings for this workload. (1) Check whether headroom exposes a way to keep the T-080 fix (Read/Grep/Write/Edit always protected, protect_recent_reads_fraction=0.0) without forcing is_cache_mode()'s strict all-but-latest-turn freeze — e.g. token mode + a custom exclude_tools/protect_recent_reads_fraction override, bypassing the mode dichotomy entirely. (2) If no such config exists upstream, decide consciously: accept near-zero compression as the cost of prefix-cache safety, or file upstream against headroom-ai for a narrower knob. (3) Update .agentflow/headroom_eval.md and architecture.md's Headroom section with the real, measured trade-off — the 60%+ vendor claim and the original T-071 integration decision were made without knowing cache mode's actual scope.

tasks.title: Calibrate handoff/session-recycling savings from real ledger data, add as report line item
tasks.description: architecture.md's Handoff (context cycling) model uses an invented 500 tok/turn constant to project ~40% savings — never measured, and design_status.md flags session-recycling as entirely missing from combined_report.html's get_bucketed_stats buckets. Oracle re-spar 2026-07-02 resolved the approach: don't build new per-turn input-token logging (too much new scope for the value); instead recalibrate the existing formula from the 99 real closed sessions already in agentflow_ledger.json. (1) New module agentflow/reporting/handoff_savings.py: for each closed session compute (final_ctx - initial_ctx) / n_turns, bucket by session_type (oracle/orchestrator). (2) Use the p25 percentile (not median) as the calibrated tok/turn constant — conservative bias per user direction, savings claims should undershoot for customer bill-reconciliation credibility, not overshoot. (3) Feed the calibrated constant into the existing triangular-sum handoff model to produce a session-recycling savings figure; wire into report_builder.py as a new line item (keep report_builder.py itself under the 250-line cap — extend via the new module, not inline). (4) The report line item must carry an explicit methodology label ('modeled from N=99 measured sessions, p25 conservative percentile, triangular-sum projection') — never presented as directly-measured, matching the aggregate-vs-waste labeling split T-083 established. (5) Update architecture.md's Handoff section to cite the calibrated constant and methodology instead of the assumed 500 tok/turn.

tasks.title: Move headroom-wrap toggle from ambient env var into AgentFlow config; add startup active/inactive banner
tasks.description: Incident 2026-07-02: a live PTY session showed zero headroom compression for hours (confirmed via lsof — claude connected directly to Anthropic edge IPs on :443, no local proxy in the path; .headroom/proxy_savings.json history had zero new entries since before the session started). Root cause: cmd_shell() (agentflow/cli.py:117) only invokes `headroom wrap <cmd>` if `os.environ.get("AGENTFLOW_ENABLE_HEADROOM")` is truthy in whatever shell happened to launch `python -m agentflow shell` — the one remaining ambient-environment dependency in an otherwise self-contained setup (HEADROOM_WORKSPACE_DIR and HEADROOM_MODE are already set unconditionally by cmd_shell itself once the gate passes). If that shell never exported the var, cmd_shell silently falls back to bare `claude` with zero indication — this is the second silent headroom-disabled incident (see T-084/T-080 history). (1) Add a `headroom.enabled` field (bool, default true) to agentflow/config/schema.py + defaults.yaml — config-owned, not shell-ambient. cmd_shell wraps whenever this resolves true AND shutil.which("headroom") succeeds. (2) Keep AGENTFLOW_ENABLE_HEADROOM as an explicit override only (env var wins over config if set), for anyone who wants to force-disable without editing config. (3) On PTY startup, print one line stating whether headroom wrap is active or inactive (and why, if inactive: not installed vs config-disabled vs env-override) — belt-and-suspenders so a future gap is visible, not silent. (4) Tests: config default-on behavior, env-override-wins behavior, and the startup banner text for all three inactive reasons.

tasks.title: Add steady-state (post-regression-fix) savings percentage alongside lifetime total in report_builder.py
tasks.description: Oracle re-spar 2026-07-02: combined_report.html currently reports one lifetime pct_saved blended across full ledger history, including the T-084 cache-mode regression window (compression steady-state <1%) and the T-086 silent-headroom-off window (compression 0 for hours) -- both real degradations, not measurement bugs, so they cannot be retroactively backfilled (see T-084/T-086). A single blended percentage understates current system capability for demo/investor use. (1) Add a second computed figure in report_builder.py: steady-state pct_saved, scoped to sessions closed after the later of T-084s and T-086s merge timestamps (fixes trading correctness), using the same total_saved formula as the lifetime figure. (2) Keep the existing lifetime pct_saved (full history) unchanged -- do not remove it, it is the more auditable/credible number and shows the regression-and-recovery trend. (3) Render both in combined_report.html: lifetime figure stays in Real Savings Realized; steady-state figure added as its own clearly-labeled stat (e.g. "Current / Steady-State Savings") near the top summary stats, not buried in the details section. (4) Keep report_builder.py under the 250-line cap -- extend via a small helper (e.g. steady_state.py in agentflow/reporting/) rather than growing report_builder.py inline, matching the T-085 pattern.

tasks.title: Surface index-driven read savings as its own report line item
tasks.description: Oracle re-spar 2026-07-02: report_builder.py computes file_reads_saved (baseline=25%-of-file vs actual bytes read, lines 162-184) across every read entry and rolls it into total_saved, but never surfaces it as its own row — the strategy list shows waste buckets (idx/no-reread/indexing-gap/state-docs) individually but the realized savings side is invisible, anonymously folded into the 'Total Tokens Saved' headline. Also file_reads_saved currently conflates idx-driven reduction with any generic offset/limit reduction (idx not required). (1) Split the per-entry loop into two accumulators: idx_savings (entries where idx_exists=True) vs offset_savings (entries with offset/limit but no idx) — same baseline/real formula, partitioned by idx_exists. (2) Add 'Targeted Reads — Savings Realized (idx)' as a new row under Real Savings Realized in dashboard_template.html and report_builder.py's template substitution, using idx_savings. (3) total_saved formula unchanged (idx_savings + offset_savings must still sum to the existing file_reads_saved so Total Tokens Saved doesn't shift). (4) Label clearly as measured (not modeled) — matches T-083's real-savings-vs-waste labeling split.
